Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Krishna is the doer

Event Date

10 Apr 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Anil Bankade’s residence

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Manoj Singh Kushwaha

Completed

18 Mar 2021

Source Audio

http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-039-Krishna%20Is%20The%20Doer%20Anil%20Bankade's%20Residence%20(Mumbai)-HP-dt_90_04_10.mp3

Source Video

Verse

BG 3.27, BG 7.26, BG 10.8, SB 1.3.43,

Tags – Places

Kurukshetra, Dwarka.

Tags – Pastimes

Krishna’s final pastimes, Draupadi’s swayamvara, Khandava forest fire, Krishna saving pandavas from Durvasa muni.

Tags – Persons

Sri Krishna, Arjun, Yudhistira, Draupadi, Karna, Dhristdyumna, Maya Danav, Dushasana, Duryodhana, Durvasa Muni, Srimati Radharani, Bhimsena, Mother Teresa.

Tags – Themes

Surrender, Mercy, Blessings, Protector.

Lecture Transcription

Arjuna is the third of the sons of Pandu. He was the recipient of the special mercy of the Supreme Lord, Bhagavan Sri Krishna. For it was to him that the most sacred teaching of Bhagavad-gita was imparted. It was while standing between the two armies of the Kurus and the Pandavas on that sacred field of Kurukshetra that Lord Sri Krishna spoke the model song that will attract all living beings’ minds back to their eternal home, the kingdom of God. Anyone who remembers the teachings of Gita in any circumstance of life will be relieved from all anxieties and miseries of the material existence and find the true path toward the truth. It is described in Gita,

prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni
guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ
ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā
kartāham iti manyate

[Bg. 3.27]

[The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.]

The basic principle of all bondage and suffering is that the foolish bewildered spirit soul thinks himself to be the doer of his activities. He does not know, he does not understand because he fails to want to understand that whatever he has in regard to his wealth, to his intelligence, to his abilities in this world they are all the gifts of God. When you understand that they are the gifts of God, then you will utilize everything God has given you very carefully in harmony with his desire, in his loving service, but when you fail to understand this principle, then you act independently for your own selfish motivations and thus you implicate yourself in infinite varieties of bondage and suffering. There is a beautiful story in the Srimad-Bhagavatam which very beautifully illustrates how the supreme Lord is always worshiped by his devotee in the mood of great humility.

We all have our abilities in this world but the greatest man on the entire planet five thousand years ago was Arjuna. He was the personal intimate friend of Sri Krishna himself. It is described that after that great battle of Kurukshetra, after Maharaja Yudhisthira was enthroned as the king, Arjuna was sent by Yudhisthira Maharaja to Dwarka to offer his respect and his obeisances to Lord Sri Krishna, Dwarkadhish as well as to find out the welfare of all of the great personalities of the Yadu Dynasty. Arjuna was away for one month, two months, three months. Yudhisthira Maharaja became very worried. There had not been any message; there had not been any word. He was away for four months, five months, and six months. At that time, Maharaja Yudhisthira became very concerned and on the seventh month, Yudhisthira Maharaja saw all inauspicious signs throughout the atmosphere and then Arjuna returned.

But when he returned, he was a man that Yudhisthira Maharaja could not recognize. That gallant heroic Arjuna, who was always full of vibrant enthusiasm, was not the same. He was pale white; his eyes were brimming with tears. He was without any luster. His head was turned toward the ground. Indeed, he looked like his heart was experiencing the voidness of the loss of one’s most intimate lover and friend. Maharaja Yudhisthira inquired from Arjuna in various ways, why he was in such a situation and Arjuna began to speak.

Arjuna laments for Krishna and remembers Krishna’s blessings

He said, “That great personality who was the friend and benefactor of all living beings, who was the intimate object of all my love, he has left me in the pangs of separation. Closing the final chapter of his earthly lila, he has returned to his eternal abode.” Arjuna explained to Yudhisthira Maharaja, “And now that Krishna has left, I am alone with nothing and I am nothing. At one time, all the kings on the earth and all the demigods in the heavens, they offered me respects., knowing me to be the most powerful of all heroes on earth. But after Krishna has left this world, he has taken away all of my powers along with him.” Arjuna explained that, “Now I know that it was only by the mercy of Krishna that I can do anything.” And this is a fact which applies to the lives of all living beings. We should not be…we should not be proud of borrowed plumes. We should not think that what God has given us on loan is ours to enjoy. Just as he gives, he can also withdraw. Just like there is the powerhouse and there are so many electrical devices that are depending on the powerhouse. Here, this evening, we see there are many very bright lights and, in your home, there are some not-so-bright light bulbs. Some light bulbs are very powerful and some light bulbs are very weak. But all the energy is coming from the power-house and if the power-house withdraws its energy, all light bulbs, however great or small they are, they all go out instantly. They are completely dependent on the energy of the power-house. As soon as the light bulb thinks that it is independent of the power-house, that that light is his, however bright or dim it may be, that light bulb is in maya or illusion. So similarly, however great or small we may be in our intelligence and our abilities, we should know that it is all coming from Krishna. And as soon as we think that we are independent of Krishna, we are in illusion. Because at any time Krishna can withdraw it and he will withdraw it in due course of time. Arjuna began to reflect on how Krishna protected him and showed his great kindness upon him throughout his life. With tears of love in his eyes and with his voice choked up in the agony of separation, he began to remember, “My dear Yudhisthira Maharaj, do you remember the swayamvara ceremony of Draupadi?”

Draupadi was the most beautiful of all princesses in the entire creation. In fact, Arjuna, by the grace of Sri Krishna, was able to defeat King Drupada in battle under the order of Dronacharya. And at that time Maharaja Drupada and all of his armies being defeated, the king was so impressed by Arjuna’s heroic kshatriya abilities that he performed a sacrifice, especially to have a daughter that would be fit to marry Arjuna. But being embarrassed by Drona during this battle, he also wanted a son that would kill Drona. So, he performed the Agnihotra and the brahmanas chanting special mantras, from the fire was born Draupadi and the mantra that was given was ‘May a beautiful woman come out that would be fit to be the bride of Arjuna’ and then the next… and then the other oblation was ‘May a great hero come out that will be the killer of Drona.’ So, from her very birth, born of the fire, Draupadi was always anxious to marry Arjuna and when she held her svayamvara ceremony, it was a ceremony where all the princes and the kings who wanted the hand of this beautiful princess had to prove their superior skills as a kshatriya. And they arranged the challenge that only Arjuna could fulfill.

They put a fish on the ceiling and it was protected by a moving wheel and the contestant could not look upward but he had to look down at the floor, where there was a vessel of water. And that water was always being moved so it was wavy. And in that moving water looking down, he had to point his arrow up and pierce the eye of the fish. All the great kings and all the great princes and all the great heroes of the world came to have Draupadi’s hand in marriage because she was the most beautiful, the most chaste, the most virtuous of all females. At that time, Dhrstadyumna was approached by Draupadi. She was in anxiety. She said, “I only want Arjuna as my husband. He is the only one. But there’s somebody else here who has the ability to pierce the eye of the fish and that is Karna. But he is born of a low class, he is sudra. Therefore, you should tell him that he is not allowed to be in this contest; only first class kshatriyas are allowed to be in this contest.” So, Karna became very angry.

And Arjuna, he explained to Yudhisthira, “By Krishna’s mercy alone was I able to pierce the eye of that fish and after taking Draupadi, Karna led all the other princes and kings to attack me to fight and by the mercy of Krishna alone I single-handedly defeated every one of them and was victorious.” So, by the mercy of Sri Krishna, when there was a great fire in the Khandava forest, at that time, the demon Maya Danava was about to be burnt. Not only was he about to be burnt but he was about to be destroyed by the disc of Krishna. And this demon came and took shelter of Arjuna’s lotus feet. It is said that sometimes the devotee is more merciful than the Lord himself. Arjuna was very kind upon this demon after he surrendered to him. And therefore, he was saved from the fire and he was saved from Krishna. When Krishna saw that this demon is taking shelter of my devotee, Krishna understood that what is dear to my devotee is dear to me even if he is a demon. And then Maya Danava begged Arjuna, “Please let me give you some gift as an expression of my appreciation.” And as a gift he built a beautiful assembly house for the Pandavas. And the assembly house that he built was like nothing else in the entire creation. It was so full of mystical magical things that princes and kings from all over the universe came just to see the assembly house of Yudhisthira. Sometimes demons are very, very expert at creating things in this material world, just like the great cities of the world. Essentially, they are built out of the modes of greed and lust. But the expert devotees of the Lord like Arjuna, they know how to even engage the demons in doing wonderful service with their abilities, for the supreme Lord.

We can use anything in Krishna’s service. Just like this video recorder that is recording the gathering this evening. What most people do with video recordings; can you imagine what they record? But that same video recorder, if it is used to record the beautiful glorification of the supreme Lord then it is transcendentally situated. Everything is potentially spiritual if we know how to connect something in the service of Krishna. So, Maya Danava who was such a very envious demon, he was engaged by Arjuna in building a beautiful, beautiful assembly house in the service of Krishna. Arjuna reminded Yudhisthira, “This is only by Krishna’s mercy that this is possible. And that same Lord Sri Krishna, when he saw Draupadi in the assembly of the Kauravas, her hair being loosened by Duhshasana, she fell at Krishna’s lotus feet knowing that he was the only shelter in the entire creation that she could turn to and with tears in her eyes, she cried out “He Krishna! He Govinda!” and that same Lord Sri Krishna protected our wife’s chastity by performing the great miracle of incarnating himself as an unlimited saree.” Krishna can tolerate any offence to Himself. After all, the father can tolerate the offence of a foolish son. But Krishna will not tolerate any offence to his devotees. It is said in English ‘Love me, love my dog.’ If you love Krishna, you must show your love to his devotee. Because devotee is like a dog; he is always loyal in his service to the supreme Lord. So, when Krishna saw that the hair of Draupadi was loosened by Duhshasana, he decided right then and there that Duhshasana and anyone who is connected to him, all their wives will have their hair loosened. And how did that take place? Arjuna was remembering, “On the great battle field of Kurukshetra, Duhshasana and all his brothers and all those connected to him were killed and all the wives had to loosen their hair as widows. It was only by the mercy of Krishna that this was possible.”

“Then one time, Durvasa Muni, by the arrangement of Duryodhana, he came to cause great trouble to us”, Arjuna was remembering. Durvasa Muni was an incarnation of Lord Shiva, a partial expansion born of Atri Muni. And it is described that Durvasa Muni was very easily pleased and very easily angered and everyone knew like this. When he came to the…to the palace of King Kuntibhoja, a very, very young girl, her name was Kumari Kunti, she pleased him with nice service and Durvasa Muni was so happy with her that he granted her the benediction in the form of a mantra and if she chanted this mantra with any name of a demigod, the demigod will appear. This is an example how he was easily pleased. Also, one time he came to Vrindavana and Sri Radha Rani, when she saw him, she was very kind to him. He is a very, very austere looking yogi and the other gopis and sakhis, they saw him and they became very afraid and they all ran away with his matted hair and he was very austere looking. He does not look like a very friendly fellow to most people. So, the gopis and the sakhis, they ran away but Srimati Radharani understood this is a guest, I must take care of him and she gave him such special care and kindness and served him with such sweetness of love that Durvasa Muni was very pleased and he granted her the benediction that whatever you cook will taste sweeter than nectar and whoever tastes your cooking will never fall ill. And when mother Yashoda heard about this benediction, she called Sri Radharani to cook for Krishna everyday so that he would never fall ill. So, Durvasa Muni was very easily pleased. But he was also very easily angered. As we know the story of Maharaja Ambarish. Simply because Maharaja Ambarish drank one drop of charanamrita before Durvasa Muni took his meal. He created a fire demon that was about to destroy the king.

So, one day Durvasa Muni went to the palace of Maharaja Duryodhana. And Duryodhana was very clever. He just served Durvasa Muni with such attention that Durvasa Muni became very pleased and he granted him any benediction. Duryodhana, he said, “Ah! you want to offer me a benediction? I want you to show kindness to my cousin, Maharaja Yudhisthira. He is now in exile in the forest. I want you to go to his place. I’ll tell you where it is and I will tell you what time you should go and you should go there for prasad.” Now, Durvasa Muni had ten thousand disciples. And wherever he would go, he would travel with thousands and thousands of disciples. So, by Yudhis…Duryuodhana’s very clever planning, Durvasa muni arrived with all of his disciples at the hermitage of Yudhisthira in the forest just after they had already finished their meals. When he came, Yudhisthira greeted him and he said, “Oh! We are very happy that you have come. Now please take your bath and then come back and take prasad.” And Durvasa Muni said, “Yes, yes we will all take our bath in the river.” So, while they were taking bath in the river, Yudhisthira Maharaja was thinking ‘My god, what we will going to do?’ He said, “Draupadi have you eaten yet?” She said, “Yes, I have eaten.” Yudhisthira said, “Then we are finished.” Because Draupadi had a special plate that was given to her by Surya, the Sun god and the benediction of this plate was that it would supply enough food for any amount of people that would come. If hundreds and millions of people would come to their house, that plate would supply enough food to perfectly satisfy hundreds and millions of people. But as soon as she ate off that plate, for the rest of the day it was an ordinary plate. And she had already finished. So, what are we going to do? Durvasa Muni will certainly curse us like anything if we do not have enough to feed all of his disciples. So Draupadi was feeling very great anxiety. And in that anxiety, she understood that there is nothing my husband could do to help me again. So again, she turned to Krishna and she began to pray, “Krishna, in this situation there is no other shelter than you.” And Krishna, who is the Lord seated within everyone’s heart, knows everything. He knows past, present and future.

vedāhaṁ samatītāni
vartamānāni cārjuna
bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni
māṁ tu veda na kaścana

[Bg. 7.26]

[O Arjuna, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I know everything that has happened in the past, all that is happening in the present, and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities; but Me no one knows.]

He also knows every living being. But him, no one can know in full. So, when his devotee sincerely prays to him, you must have complete faith that Krishna is hearing that prayer. But you should also have faith that whenever you speak nonsense, Krishna also hears that. He hears everything. In Gita, thirteenth chapter, Krishna says, everywhere are my eyes and ears and hands, and legs. I see everything, I hear everything, I taste everything, I feel everything. Nothing goes unnoticed by the eyes and the ears of the super-soul.

So Draupadi began to pray. Lord Krishna please save us and Krishna, right at that time happened to appear at the door of the hermitage of Maharaja Yudhisthira. And when Draupadi saw him, she was very glad to see him. And he said, “I have come. Now, you have some prasad for me?” Draupadi said, “There’s no prasad, everything is finished. I have already taken my meals, there is nothing here.” And she was feeling so bad. Not only I am going to offend Durvasa Muni and all of his followers but I don’t even have anything for Krishna. And she began to cry, tears flowing from her eyes, in a very emotional state, explaining to Krishna the helpless condition she was in. Then Krishna said, “Ah! Get your pots, just let us see if there is anything in the pots left.” So Draupadi very obediently brought the pot and there was one tiny little speck of vegetable stuck to the bottom of the pot. Then Krishna took that subji and he ate it. Now when Krishna is satisfied, it is like satisfying the root of the tree, every part of the tree is satisfied, mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ [Bg. 7.26]. And every living being is part of Krishna. So, after he took this particle of vegetable, all of the disciples of Durvasa Muni including Durvasa himself, their stomachs became very, very full, as if they just ate a gigantic feast. They didn’t know why is this happening. And then Draupadi told Bhima, “Bhimasena, go and call Durvasa’s disciples.” So, Bhimasena went to the river. He said, “Why are you delaying? Prasad is ready for you. Come!” They began to think ‘Yudhisthira is such a great gentleman, he is such an honorable man. If we go there, he surely must have just a very spectacular feast prepared for us and if we cannot honor any of it, [indistinct] it will be very embarrassing. So, they all ran away. Arjuna was remembering, how Krishna protected us.

He was remembering how even Lord Shiva in the guise of a hunter challenged Arjuna and Arjuna astounded Lord Shankar by his tremendous ability for fighting and Shiva gave him great benedictions. Arjuna also was able to, in his self-same body, go to the heavenly planets, where he was given an elevated seat beside Indra. It is also explained that Indra, when he was attacked by a very powerful demon, he and all the demigods begged Arjuna to destroy this demon because they did not have power to do so. It was all by the mercy of Sri Krishna. And by the mercy of Sri Krishna, Arjuna was remembering, during the final year of his exile, at the palace of King Virat, all the great Kuru chiefs attacked to steal the cows and the young child of the king. He was alone to fight against all these heroes and Arjuna, who was in the disguise of a dance teacher, he drove the chariot and when they saw the insurmountable ocean of the great chiefs like Bhisma and Drona and Salya…um… and Karna and Kripa, they became very fearful. But Arjuna was able to defeat all of them and protect the cows. Not only was he able to defeat them but he was able to take the jewels and the crowns from their very bodies.

Arjuna was remembering that ‘Here I am, the same Gandiva Bow, the same arrows, the same chariot, the same horses but now that Krishna has left, everything I have is null and void. After Krishna left this world, I went to Dwaraka and I was attempting to protect the queens of Lord Sri Krishna and at that time abandoned infidel cowherd men came to steal the queens. And I took my Gandiva bow to defend and they completely defeated me and powerless as I was, they stole the queens of Krishna.’ Of course, we understand the esoteric significance of this story that those cowherd men were actually incarnations of Krishna himself to steal his own queens. But Arjuna was not aware of this. Therefore, Arjuna was feeling himself in a completely morose and destitute situation and then he began to remember the teachings of Krishna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, the teachings of the Bhagavad-gita. And as soon as he remembered those instructions of Sri Krishna, again there was courage, again there was strength and again there was enthusiasm to live.

Bhagavad-gita was not simply spoken to Arjuna. Bhagavad-gita was spoken to all living beings, to all humanity. And it is a fact that in any situation of life, even in the most difficult reversals, even in the most seemingly impossible conditions, if we remember the teachings of Sri Krishna as he spoke Bhagavad-gita, we will get courage, we will get strength, we will get direction, and we will get life. Why is this? Because the name of Krishna and the instruction of Krishna is non-different than Krishna. The Bhagavad-gita is sound vibration of Krishna Himself. He has incarnated in that sound. And similarly, he has incarnated in the sound of his holy name. This is the very essence of spiritual life and anyone who wants to genuinely make spiritual advancement, they have to understand this narration we are speaking tonight.

The first principle of spiritual life is you must understand that you are nothing without the mercy of God. And if you think you are anything, you are the greatest fool. Whatever youth, whatever strength, whatever wealth, whatever abilities, whatever intelligence, it is all borrowed by Krishna… borrowed from Krishna. And if we become proud of anything, we are utterly and totally disqualified from spiritual life. Therefore, we must try to understand the glory of Krishna and be free from this disease of pride. That is the first principle of spiritual life. That we are all completely depending on the mercy of the Lord. That everything is coming from him.

ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo
mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate
iti matvā bhajante māṁ
budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ

[Bg. 10.8]

[I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service and worship Me with all their hearts.]

Krishna says, “I am the source of all material and spiritual worlds. Everything is emanating from me.” And this is the very essential principle of all the great religions. Even Lord Jesus Christ, when he would perform great miracles, he would say, “It is not me that is doing this, but it is the father in heaven who does this through me.”

I saw one interview of Mother Teresa and this newspaper journalist was praising her activities like anything and she was completely indifferent, everything he was saying. He was just praising her, and praising her mission, and praising her kindness upon these poor destitute people and she just looked at him like it had nothing to do with her. She just said, “It is all God’s work.” When our spiritual master, His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, when he single-handedly at the age of seventy years old, without any money or any assistance from anyone on this earth, he started a world-wide movement with hundreds of temples, millions of books being distributed, tens and thousands of disciples, he remained very simple and humble and if anyone would praise him, he would simply say that it is all the work of my spiritual master. He said, “He is so kind that he is sending so many people to assist. I am doing nothing.” This is the mood of the great souls. In success or failure, in victory or defeat, in happiness or distress, the great souls are always seeing the merciful hand of God. We are all depending on Krishna for the air we breathe, for the sunlight, for everything. And therefore, with this understanding we can humbly chant the name of God. We can take shelter of the name of God, which is non-different than Krishna Himself, kali-kāle nāma-rūpe kṛṣṇa-avatāra [Cc. Adi 17.22]. Krishna incarnates in this age of Kali as his name. He incarnates as the Shastra. It is described,

kṛṣṇe sva-dhāmopagate
dharma-jṣānādibhiḥ saha
kalau naṣṭa-dṛśām eṣa
purāṇārko ’dhunoditaḥ

[SB 1.3.43]

[This Bhāgavata Purāṇa is as brilliant as the sun, and it has arisen just after the departure of Lord Kṛṣṇa to His own abode, accompanied by religion, knowledge, etc. Persons who have lost their vision due to the dense darkness of ignorance in the Age of Kali shall get light from this Purāṇa.]

Complete surrender unto Krishna’s mercy and his holy Name

that Krishna has incarnated in the form of the Srimad-Bhagavatam and the Bhagavad-gita. And factually just as Krishna spoke to Arjuna on the chariot five thousand years ago, when you read Bhagavad-gita, Krishna is speaking to you, exactly in the same way. There is no difference. There…The only difference is Arjuna was humble and submissive and we are not. Because the vibration of that book, the vibration coming from the lips of the saints who are teaching Gita is absolutely non-different than what Arjuna experienced. Arjuna was submissive. Arjuna humbled himself before God and therefore he was completely enlightened by the teachings of the Gita. And even in the most distressful calamities in life, when he simply remembered Krishna in humility, when he remembered those instructions, he was enlightened. If we hear Gita with that submission, we will get the exact result as Arjuna did. There is no difference. It is an eternal message.

So, by hearing the glories of the Lord, by hearing the words of the Lord and by chanting the names of the Lord, understanding how much we are dependent completely on his mercy, this quality alone will deliver you from the cycle of birth and death. In the age of Kali-yuga, there is only one means of returning to our original spiritual position and that is, chanting the name of God in utter humility, completely depending on the mercy of the holy name, completely taking shelter of the instruction coming from the guru and Krishna. If we simply take shelter of the instruction coming through guru, who is the representative of God, speaking Gita, and if we completely learn to take shelter of the name of God, as simple as it sounds, there is no doubt, it is Krishna’s promise, that we will attain him without fail. All the miseries and problems of this world, there are so many grand arrangements to try to make things better. But everything is getting worse because people have missed the real point. Until we put God in the center and recognize we are depending on him, until we put him on…in the center by chanting his name, by coming together to glorify him, there is no real hope for peace in this world because all attempts are artificial and superficial. We must base our pursuit for happiness on the truth. Krishna says in Gita that, “The truth is to know that all living beings are part of me, they are depending on me and that they are mine.” So, let us chant the holy names of the Lord with great sincerity. Whether you are old or young, rich or poor, wealthy in knowledge or illiterate, man or woman, Western or Eastern, in any situation in life, if you simply develop this quality of consciousness, then your life will be perfect. hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare.

Let us follow in the footsteps of Arjuna and always be… [break]
